checks.name: arena: m == 84 edges, degree histogram {2: 4 corners, 3: 20 boundary, 4: 25 interior}, sum deg == 2m == 168
Matrix-Tree sanity: #spanning trees == 19872369301840986112 == OEIS A007341(7) (exact integer Bareiss determinant of the 48x48 minor)
1. every row of B is a +1/-1 dipole spike pair: +1 at the tail, -1 at the head, tail index < head index (lex orientation), exactly 2 nonzeros, row sums 0 (all 84 rows)
2a. X == pinv(B^T) == B L^+ (response matrix; pinv computed independently of L^+ -- the 1-D version is dual_basis_explainer's X)
2b. every row e of X == L^+ b_e: the potential field of a unit dipole (+1 tail, -1 head) across edge e, in the mean-zero gauge (all 84 rows)
2c. every response row is BALANCED: row sums == 0 (free graph -> fields live perpendicular to the constant gauge mode)
2d. X^T X == L^+ (covariance = Gram of the dense responses, free version)
2e. X^T B == I - 11^T/49: biorthogonality modulo the gauge (the centering projector, exactly dual_basis' free-path P8 note, now 2-D)
3. every column v of B hits exactly deg(v) edges: +1 where v is the tail, -1 where v is the head (the incident-edge stencil; all 49 columns)
4a. column v of X == B (L^+ e_v): Ohm's law currents of the Green potential field -- the unit-load / Maxwell picture on edges (all 49 cols)
4b. divergence B^T X == I - 11^T/49: injection +1 at v is balanced by the gauge's uniform extraction -1/49 everywhere (Kirchhoff on the free graph)
5. L == B^T B has the stencil structure exactly: L[v,v] == deg(v) (2/3/4), L[v,w] == -1 iff v ~ w, 0 otherwise (conditional / regression reading of row v)

6a. sign rule of the EDGE LAPLACIAN verified structurally: diag == 2 (unit conductances), off-diag +1 when edges converge/diverge at the shared vertex, -1 when they form a directed path through it (72 pairs +1, 142 pairs -1, 214 == sum_v C(deg v, 2) == 214 adjacent pairs)
6b. line-graph identity: |B| |B|^T == 2I + A(line graph) -- unsigned version; 'edge Laplacian' is legible as 2I + signed line-graph adjacency

6c. nonzero spectra of L and L_edge COINCIDE with multiplicity (48 shared eigenvalues, max gap 6.22e-15); L has 1 zero (constants), L_edge has 36 zeros == m - n + 1 == 36 == dim(cycle space) -- why 'edge Laplacian' deserves the name
6d. spectrum closed form: eig(L) == {4 sin^2(i pi/14) + 4 sin^2(j pi/14)} (P7 [] P7 Cartesian product), lambda_2 = 0.198062, lambda_max = 7.60388
7a. L^+ symmetric with L^+ 1 == 0: every Green column lives in the mean-zero gauge (grounding_explainer's pinv row, now a 2-D field)
7b. L L^+ == I - 11^T/49: column v of L^+ is the potential field of injection +1 at v, extraction 1/49 everywhere (the free-graph Green function identity)
8a. L_edge^+ == X X^T: the pseudoinverse of the edge Laplacian is the GRAM OF THE RESPONSES -- entry (e,f) = <dipole field e, dipole field f> = dipole-to-dipole transfer in the resistance metric
8b. L_edge L_edge^+ == Pi: inverting the edge Laplacian recovers exactly Vishnoi's projection (modes 6, 8, 9 are one story)
9a. Pi == B L^+ B^T == X B^T (cross-Gram of measurements and responses)
9b. Pi symmetric idempotent (Pi^2 == Pi): orthogonal projection on edge space
9c. spec(Pi) == {0 x 36, 1 x 48}: rank == n - 1 == 48, corank == m - n + 1 == 36 (cut space + cycle space = edge space)
9d. diag(Pi) == effective resistances b_e^T L^+ b_e == leverage scores of the 84 edge rows
9e. Foster's theorem: sum_e Reff(e) == tr Pi == n - 1 == 48
9f. Thm 4.5 by EXACT Matrix-Tree deletion: P[e in T] == (N(G) - N(G-e))/N(G) == Reff(e) for all 84 edges (integer determinants, zero-tolerance fractions vs float Reff)
9g. exact marginals sum: sum_e P[e in T] == 48 EXACTLY in Fractions (every spanning tree has 48 edges)

9h. explicit face cycle killed by Pi: the square 24->25->32->31->24 has B^T c == 0 (divergence-free) and Pi c == 0, (I - Pi) c == c -- a cycle current is invisible to Pi (the page's mini-visualization)
9i. ALL 36 face cycles are killed by Pi and span ker(Pi): rank(faces) == 36 == corank(Pi) (I - Pi projects onto the cycle space)
10a. Neumann / path expansion: G_g == sum_k (D^-1 W)^k D^-1 restricted off g, summed to K = 6597 (rel err 1.2e-14); convergence guaranteed by rho == 0.994702 < 1
10b. measured decay rate of the partial-sum error == rho: fitted 0.994702 vs rho(D^-1 W|off g) = 0.994702 (even-K fit, bipartite grid; tau_relax = -1/ln rho = 188.3 steps)

10c. absorbing-chain recurrence EXACT in Fractions: visits(i->j) == delta_ij + sum_k P(i->k) visits(k->j) for all 48x48 pairs (N = (I-Q)^{-1} = fundamental matrix of the chain absorbed at g; start counts as a visit)
10d. visit-count identity: G_g[i,j] == E[# visits to j starting at i before absorption at g] / d_j, exact fractions vs float inverse (all 48x48 entries)
10e. reversibility bonus: d_i N[i,j] == d_j N[j,i] EXACTLY (symmetry of G_g seen through visit counts)

10f. exact expected hitting time center -> corner: E[steps 24 -> 0] == 3083931/16211 == 190.236938 (row sum of the fundamental matrix; satisfies the first-step recurrence h_i = 1 + (1/d_i) sum_(j~i) h_j exactly at all 48 vertices, and equals the pinned literal 3083931/16211)

arena.graph: 7x7 free grid, n = 49 vertices, m = 84 edges, unit conductances
arena.vertex_order: row-major v = 7r + c, r, c in 0..6; widget (x, y) = (c, r)
arena.orientation: every edge tail -> head with tail index < head index (DirectedGraph-Acyclic lex order, electrical_networks.wls convention); edge list sorted lex by (tail, head)

spectra.closed_form: eig(L) = {4 sin^2(i pi/14) + 4 sin^2(j pi/14), i,j = 0..6} (P7 x P7 Cartesian product)

1_B_row.caption: the measurement: +1 at the tail, -1 at the head

2_X_row.caption: gauge-fixed dipole response field; balanced (sums to 0); the 2-D sibling of dual_basis' centered steps

3_B_col.caption: incident-edge stencil: +1 on out-edges (v is tail), -1 on in-edges (v is head)

4_X_col.caption: internal currents from unit injection at v, balanced by the gauge's uniform -1/49 extraction (Maxwell unit load)

5_L_row.caption: vertex Gram: degree at v, -1 at neighbors (stencil / conditional reading)

6_L_edge_row.caption: EDGE LAPLACIAN row: 2 on e, +1 on edges converging/diverging at a shared vertex, -1 on directed-path neighbors; |B||B|^T = 2I + A(line graph)
6_L_edge_row.sign_rule: L_edge[e,f] = s_e(v) s_f(v) at the shared vertex v; +1 tail-tail or head-head, -1 tail-head

7_L_pinv_col.caption: free-grid Green function in the mean-zero gauge (grounding_explainer's pinv row, now 2-D)

8_L_edge_pinv_row.caption: L_edge^+ == X X^T: dipole-to-dipole transfer in the resistance metric (Gram of the responses)

9_Pi_row.caption: currents through every edge when a unit dipole drives edge e; diag = Reff = leverage = P[e in spanning tree]

cycle_demo.caption: a face-cycle current: B^T c = 0, Pi c = 0, (I - Pi) c = c -- invisible to Pi

10_walk_sum.click: vertex (source); fields baked for default s = 24, g = 0
10_walk_sum.visit_count_note: G_g[i,j] = E[# visits to j of the simple random walk started at i, before absorption at g, counting the start when i == j] / d_j (verified exactly in Fractions)
